---
title: "Week 2 — Design"
description: "Prototyping, firmware setup, and character development"
---

```{=html}
<div class="week-hero week-hero-w2">
  <div class="week-num" style="color:#A78BFA">// WEEK TWO &nbsp;·&nbsp; 第二周</div>
  <div class="week-title">Design</div>
  <div class="week-zh">设计</div>
  <p class="week-desc">
    You have a concept. Now make it real — at low fidelity first, then higher.
    Week 2 is about <strong>rapid prototyping and fearless iteration</strong>:
    electronics on a breadboard, firmware running hello-world, and a 3D sketch of the body.
  </p>
</div>
```

## Learning Objectives

By the end of Week 2 you will be able to:

- Wire a basic ESP32 circuit with a display or sensor
- Flash firmware using the ESP-Claw framework
- Send your first message to an LLM via OpenRouter and receive a response
- Sketch and prototype the robot's physical form
- Run a first user test (lo-fi prototype, real feedback)

---

## Sessions

### Session 4 — Electronics & Firmware First Contact

Get the hardware talking.

::: {.callout-note}
This is often the most intimidating session. Don't worry — by the end of it your board will be blinking, displaying text, or responding to a button press. That's real.
:::

**Agenda:**
- Intro to ESP32 GPIO, I2C, and SPI interfaces
- Wire up a display (OLED or LCD) on a breadboard
- Flash a starter ESP-Claw sketch
- Make the display show your robot's name
- Troubleshooting time with instructors

**Tools needed:**
```
ESP32 dev board + USB cable
Breadboard + jumper wires
OLED display (SSD1306) or LCD
Arduino IDE or VS Code + PlatformIO
```

---

### Session 5 — LLM Integration via OpenRouter

Connect your robot's brain to an AI model.

::: {.callout-tip title="Why OpenRouter?"}
OpenRouter provides a **single API** that routes to any LLM — Qwen, DeepSeek, Claude, and others. You write one integration and can swap models with a single parameter change. No re-wiring required.
:::

**Agenda:**
- OpenRouter account setup + API key
- HTTP request from ESP32 to OpenRouter API
- Parse JSON response and display reply on screen
- Prompt engineering: write a system prompt that gives your robot its personality
- Compare two models (e.g. Qwen vs DeepSeek) — what's different?

**Starter prompt template:**
```
You are [robot name], a [personality adjective] AI companion
designed for [target user].

You respond in [language preference], in [tone] — no more than
2 sentences per reply. You never break character.

When someone says hello, you respond with your signature greeting.
```

---

### Session 6 — Form & Character Development

Guest lecture: **JunJun Shi 史珺珺** on *"Empathy as the starting point for designing robot personality."*

**Agenda:**
- Guest lecture: how human insight shapes a robot's soul
- Workshop: translate your personality brief into physical form
- Sketch 3 body shapes — which one *feels* like your robot?
- Rapid paper prototype: cut, fold, tape — 30 minutes
- First user test: show the paper prototype to someone outside your team
- Iterate: one change based on feedback

::: {.callout-important title="Lo-fi is the point"}
Paper prototypes aren't "not done yet." They're a tool. The goal is to **learn fast and cheaply** before committing to 3D printing.
:::

---

::: {.deliverables}
#### ✓ Week 2 Deliverables

- [ ] **Working hardware sketch** — ESP32 + display showing robot name / first response
- [ ] **LLM integration demo** — robot sends a prompt and displays the reply
- [ ] **3D sketch / paper prototype** of the robot's enclosure
- [ ] **Personality system prompt** — documented, tested, iterable
- [ ] **User test notes** — what you showed, who you showed it to, what you learned
:::

---

## Resources for Week 2

- [ESP-Claw Getting Started](https://esp-claw.com/en/) — official docs
- [OpenRouter API docs](https://openrouter.ai/docs) — model list, pricing, API reference
- [Qwen model card](https://huggingface.co/Qwen) — capabilities and prompting tips
- [DeepSeek documentation](https://platform.deepseek.com/docs) — Chinese-language model strengths
- [Claude API docs](https://docs.anthropic.com) — for Claude-specific prompting

---

*← [Week 1 — Conceive](week1.qmd) &nbsp;|&nbsp; [Week 3 — Build →](week3.qmd)*
